advisory_message: Split by material immediately: have Andy place only granite/andesite/diorite/stone/stone bricks, and Bob place only gold, quartz, quartz pillars, and glowstone. Confirm distinct coordinates or layers before either continues.

Bob, retry the sole quartz placement at (-343,66,249) using your remaining block. Andy, stop broad harvesting and inspect/clear that cell or provide safe access; then have Bob place it and run checkBlueprint.

Assign Bob to place the blueprint's gold_block, quartz_block, quartz_pillar, and glowstone coordinates now, while Andy continues the masonry list. Have them confirm coordinate coverage first to avoid overlap.

Pause Bob's stale broad fix pass and refresh from Andy's 218.622s blueprint check. Split the verified remainder by inventory: Bob handles quartz_block/quartz_pillar; Andy handles stone_bricks or another explicitly assigned non-overlapping fix.

Partition the remaining build now: keep Andy on common-material fixes at Y64-Y66, and redirect Bob to Y67-Y68 plus any still-missing gold/quartz/pillar/glowstone blocks. Have Bob avoid Andy's coordinates until Andy checks completion.

Have Bob stop the broad gather/build loop, move clear of (-343,64,254), run checkBlueprint, and own only gold/quartz/quartz_pillar/glowstone fixes. Let Andy finish the andesite target; transfer it to Bob only after an explicit failed handoff.

Have Andy stop attempting (-343,66,253) and move clear. Assign Bob sole ownership because he has the quartz_block; Bob should place it and run checkBlueprint. Andy should stand by for another fix only if verification finds one.

Assign Andy to place Level 0 polished granite/andesite/diorite, stone, and stone bricks from his existing inventory. Ask Bob to stop or report on the overrun gathering action, return to gold/quartz work, and then rerun the Level 0 check.

Andy: start placing your polished granite/andesite/diorite, stone, and stone bricks at the listed Level 0 fixes now. Bob: finish only the current gather needed for shortages, then retain specialty-block placement and verification; coordinate missing counts with Andy.

Andy: immediately place the required polished granite/andesite/diorite, stone, and stone bricks from your inventory. Bob: stop duplicate stone gathering, place gold/quartz/pillar/glowstone, then run level checks and send Andy the remaining coordinate list.

Partition the build now: have Bob place gold, quartz, quartz pillars, and glowstone, and Andy place stone bricks and polished stone variants. Give them non-overlapping coordinates or levels, then run one shared blueprint check.

Partition the remaining work now: keep Bob on stocked gold/quartz/glowstone targets; assign Andy quartz pillars and stone-variant fixes only. Refresh checkBlueprint and divide exact coordinates before further placement or material gathering.

Split by inventory: have Bob place the remaining gold, quartz, quartz-pillar, and glowstone blocks. Have Andy stop sourcing those and gather/craft the remaining stone variants. Run one fresh blueprint check before either touches shared coordinates.
Partition the remaining build now: have Bob own Y65-Y66 and Andy own Y67-Y68 plus the lone Y64 fix. Tell each to avoid the other's coordinates, then run one fresh blueprint check after both passes.
Bob, pause the polished-diorite search and switch to blueprint verification. Let Andy mine the specific spare he located and place it at -343,64,251; Bob should resume only if Andy reports failure.

Andy: source/craft the stone variants from your latest check and place support cells bottom-up. Bob: while waiting, recheck the exact remaining specials and source only any shortages; retry placement after Andy confirms the supports.

Andy: immediately recheck and acquire/place the remaining level-2 stone-variant supports while Bob continues the six gold/quartz targets. Report any material or placement blocker promptly so Bob does not keep retrying unsupported cells.

Specialize now: Bob should place only the remaining gold, quartz, quartz-pillar, and glowstone fixes; Andy should gather/place stone and polished variants. Claim coordinate ranges from the latest check before acting, then run one shared recheck.

Keep Andy as the sole block editor for this pass. Have Bob avoid overlapping placement, run checkBlueprint immediately after Andy finishes, and report exact residuals; then assign Bob only material-backed fixes that remain.

Andy: immediately place the level-0 stone, stone-brick, and polished-stone blocks from your inventory, or transfer them to Bob. Bob: stop gathering those materials and focus on gold, quartz, glowstone, and verification. Confirm this split directly.

Confirm the split directly: Andy should immediately place the level-0 stone, stone-brick, and polished stone blocks from his inventory; Bob should stop gathering those materials and place only gold, quartz, pillars, and glowstone, then jointly recheck level 0.

Reassign Andy now to place the level-0 stone, stone_bricks, polished_andesite, polished_diorite, and polished_granite from his inventory. Have Bob stop duplicating stone gathering, return to the site, and handle only gold/quartz/glowstone plus a joint blueprint check.

Assign Andy to place or hand off his polished granite, andesite, and diorite for the remaining fixes. Tell Bob to skip gathering those materials and focus on gold/quartz fixes; keep Candy on stone and stone-brick work.

Partition by inventory now: Andy place only polished variants; Bob place gold/quartz/pillars. Ask Candy to report or hand off granite/cobble and, if free, own stone/stone-brick gaps. Designate one agent for the next blueprint check.

Let Andy finish his active placement run. Have Bob pause duplicate placement and own the next blueprint check plus remaining gold/quartz coordinates. Ask Candy to craft or deliver stone/polished materials, then partition the remaining coordinates before further placement.

Repartition by inventory: assign Candy only stone_bricks, stone, and glowstone coordinates; reserve Bob for gold/quartz blocks and pillars; have Andy run checkBlueprint and handle remaining granite/andesite/diorite fixes. Share remaining coordinates before placement.

Andy: use your stocked polished_diorite, stone_bricks, and other materials to place every item in Bob’s 314.5s remaining-fixes list. Bob: confirm coordinates and verify afterward. Candy: stop further scavenging once this handoff is confirmed.

At the next safe pause, partition the remaining work: Candy runs one fresh check and publishes missing coordinates; Bob exclusively places gold/quartz; Andy handles stone/stone-brick crafting and placement. Give Candy only a disjoint polished-block set or verification role.

Stop duplicate searches: have Bob place his 2 gold and 2 quartz at the remaining rare-block coordinates. Have Andy give cobblestone to Candy; Candy should smelt/craft common blocks, while Andy handles polished fixes and runs the next blueprint check.

Andy, verify your plank inventory and retry giving Bob 1 oak plank while Bob confirms receipt. If transfer fails again, Andy should craft the table from 4 planks and coordinate access so Bob can craft the pickaxe.

Reconnect/check Andy and have Andy transfer 3 of the 10 oak planks to Bob, or craft the pickaxe using Bob’s sticks. Once the handoff is confirmed, tell Bob to stop the difficult underground plank collection.

Contact Andy and have him return to Bob and transfer at least 3 oak planks; if no crafting table is available, transfer 7 so Bob can craft one and the pickaxe. Tell Bob to stop searching and regroup for the handoff.

Andy: rejoin or confirm availability, rendezvous with Bob, and transfer at least 7 oak planks so the team can make a crafting table and pickaxe head. Bob: stop broad wood searches once Andy confirms and coordinate the handoff using your sticks.
Check whether Andy can reconnect. If available, have Andy immediately craft the shears or transfer both iron ingots to Bob; pause Bob's toolless iron search until that handoff is resolved.

Check Andy's connection immediately. If available, have Andy craft shears from the 2 held ingots or transfer them to Bob. If Andy has left, reconnect or replace Andy; tell Bob to stop repeating tool-less pathfinding and hold at -10,53,58 for the handoff.

Check whether Andy can reconnect or resume. If available, have Andy immediately bring the existing 2 iron ingots to Bob at about -11,53,58 so Bob can craft shears with his table; otherwise reassign recovery of those ingots.
Andy, immediately tell Bob you have 2 iron ingots and rendezvous with him to use his crafting table and craft the shears. Bob, pause further mining and coordinate the table/iron handoff with Andy.

Bob, finish and report the current craft attempt. Andy, since you still hold both iron ingots, craft the shears directly if Bob's attempt fails; do not retry table placement. Confirm completion or explicitly redo the handoff.
Andy, stop retrying the iron handoff and craft one pair of shears directly from your two ingots. Bob, pause further craft attempts and let Andy complete the recipe.

Andy, take over recovery: verify both inventories and, if no pickaxe exists, use your planks and sticks to craft a replacement. Bob, stop retrying the transfer and confirm or provide the crafting table.
Bob, stop the second-pickaxe effort; Andy has already crafted the requested pickaxe. Andy, confirm the completed item so both agents can avoid further duplicate crafting.

Andy, place the crafting table beside Bob or give it to him. Bob, use your 10 planks and 10 sticks to craft the wooden pickaxe, then verify inventory. Do not retry the pickaxe transfer unless Andy first confirms possessing one.

Bob, preserve the 16 terracotta and 2 cyan dye while Andy investigates an alternate/manual crafting method or the recipe-tool mismatch. Avoid further identical craftRecipe retries until Andy reports a concrete workaround.

Andy, verify the 8 terracotta already in your inventory before waiting for a transfer. Bob, confirm the request and either meet Andy to test a handoff or try crafting with your own 8 terracotta and cyan dye.
